title: Scope fleet publication fingerprints to effective generator inputs
goal: Replace the over-broad RepoBrief fleet publication fingerprint with a versioned, explicit digest of effective generator/runtime inputs so test-, documentation- and timer-only Lenskit changes do not republish every repository while source, generator, contract and relevant configuration changes still invalidate safely.

acceptance.assertion: The fleet fingerprint is derived from a versioned, reviewable input contract that explicitly identifies the generator/runtime code, contracts and configuration capable of changing published bundle semantics.
Changing only tests, documentation, timer units or unrelated Lenskit modules leaves existing repository publication fingerprints unchanged.
Changing repository source SHA, effective generator code, output contracts or relevant publication configuration changes the fingerprint deterministically.
The fingerprint schema/version migration is explicit, fail-closed and auditable; malformed or unknown prior state is never silently treated as current.
Focused tests and a fleet dry-run prove zero unrelated republish candidates for a test/docs/timer-only change and expected candidates for a real generator change.
The optimization does not suppress source changes, bypass generator upgrades, weaken retention or reachability guards, or claim semantic equivalence without explicit inputs.

source_observation.interpretation: A test file under merger/lenskit changed the whole merger/lenskit tree digest and therefore invalidated every repository fingerprint, although no effective generator/runtime code changed.

validation: Focused positive and negative fingerprint invalidation tests passed.
The explicit schema migration rejects malformed or unknown prior fingerprint state instead of treating it as current.
All GitHub checks passed, including pytest-full, browser-tests, Ruff, release candidate, contracts and CodeQL.
Merge-parent readback confirmed first parent 55f841f08c3b76ab6fa5d40e9bebda6b3fb687ba and reviewed head parent fa582701f9f4b9726a23c5191acfdaf6aac8b8bb.

live_dogfood.note: The rolling path was overwritten by a later healthy hourly run; current content still proves the same 40 unchanged repositories, zero publications and unchanged generator input digest.

metadata.completion_summary: PR #1015 replaced the whole-tree fingerprint with a versioned effective-input contract. A later hourly run skipped all 40 unchanged repositories, while the T026 production run published three repositories with real source changes, proving both negative and positive invalidation paths.

verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t022-versioned-input-contract: The publisher uses the explicit versioned repobrief-generator-inputs-v1 contract covering generator/runtime code, contracts and relevant configuration.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t022-negative-invalidation: Tests prove that test-, documentation-, service-, timer- and unrelated-module changes leave the generator input digest and existing repository fingerprints stable.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t022-positive-invalidation: Tests and the T026 live run prove deterministic invalidation for source SHA and effective generator inputs; three repositories with real changes were published.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t022-migration: The new schema is explicit and fail-closed; malformed or unknown prior state is not silently accepted as current.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t022-dogfood: The current rolling receipt records 40 checked, 0 changed, 0 published, 40 skipped and 0 failed under generator input SHA 7b2256…, while the preserved T026 receipt records three expected publications.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t022-non-claims: No source suppression, generator-upgrade bypass, weakening of retention/reachability guards or semantic equivalence of unlisted inputs is claimed.
